steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The graph shows correlations between the model's predictions and P1's actual ratings: 0.57 for impressionist paintings and 0.14 for color-field paintings. A higher correlation means the model tracked P1's ratings more closely; a lower correlation means it tracked them poorly. The large gap between the two values is the key data point.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — P1 would derive less aesthetic pleasure from impressionist paintings than from color-field paintings.: correlation values show how well the model tracked P1's ratings, not which style P1 enjoyed more. ✗
  • Choice B — P1 would derive more aesthetic pleasure from impressionist paintings than from color-field paintings.: same problem — the correlations don't reveal the direction of P1's enjoyment. ✗
  • Choice C — P1's ratings for impressionist and color-field paintings would differ from one another.: the gap between 0.57 and 0.14 shows the model performed very differently on the two styles, indicating P1's actual ratings differed between them. ✓
  • Choice D — P1's ratings for impressionist and color-field paintings would equal one another.: directly contradicted by the different correlation values. ✗
C is correct.
